# VantaUI CSS: Complete Reference

> A dark, angular, military-telemetry **tactical HUD** CSS library. Semantic-first, similar to BeerCSS: you write standard, plain semantic HTML to get a tactical UI without using component classes. Reach for one short helper word only to deviate. Responsive by default, OKLCH colors, zero runtime dependencies, framework-agnostic (Vue / Nuxt / vanilla). Optional tiny JS adds tabs, animated meters, drawers, dropdowns, toasts, tooltips, a live clock.

This is the complete, single-file reference. Reading it in full is sufficient to use the library correctly without following any links. For a lean index linking to focused topic files, see https://vantaui-css.khodok.com/llms.txt.

## Critical facts (read first)

- **The package name is `vantaui-css`.** It is not published to npm or any CDN yet, so you should install it directly from GitHub (`github:Khoding/vantaui-css`). All import specifiers start with `vantaui-css`.
- **You opt in with one class: `class="vui"`** on a root element (usually `<body>`). Inside `.vui`, plain semantic HTML is styled automatically. Outside `.vui`, only design tokens are defined, so nothing changes globally.
- **Semantic-first, almost zero classes.** A `<header>` containing a `<nav>` is an app bar. An `<article>` is a panel. A `<dialog class="left">` is a drawer. A `<meter>` is a telemetry bar. You rarely add classes; when you do, it is one short helper word (`glow`, `danger`, `small`, `left`).
- **All VantaUI styles live in cascade layers and are authored at zero specificity (`:where()`).** Your app's own (unlayered) classes ALWAYS win. You never need `!important` to override VantaUI.
- **`<i>text</i>` is an ICON** (Material Symbols ligature), e.g. `<i>home</i>` draws the home glyph. Use `<em>` for italic text, never `<i>`.
- Two prefixes exist: bare semantic styling needs **no prefix**; free-floating layout & utility helpers use the **`vui-`** prefix (collision-safe), e.g. `vui-grid`, `vui-p-4`, `vui-text-cyan`.
- CSS is fully usable with **no JavaScript**. JS only adds interactive niceties.

## Install

Since it is not on npm or a CDN yet, you can install it directly from GitHub. This adds a dependency that resolves like `"vantaui-css": "github:Khoding/vantaui-css"`:

```bash
npm i github:Khoding/vantaui-css
```

```js
import 'vantaui-css';                     // the stylesheet (package "main"/"style")
import { init } from 'vantaui-css/js';    // optional behaviours
init();                                   // boots tabs, animated meters, drawers, menus, toolbars, tooltips, clock, carousels
```

```html
<!-- Optional faster icon path (also bundled via @import in the CSS): -->
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Material+Symbols+Outlined:opsz,wght,FILL,GRAD@24,400,0,0&display=block" />
<body class="vui">…</body>
```

Fonts (Chakra Petch · Rajdhani · Share Tech Mono · Material Symbols) load automatically via `@import` inside the stylesheet. Note that the remote `@import` can break some build tools (like LightningCSS). If this occurs, you can vendor a copy of the stylesheet with the `@import` line removed and load the fonts separately.

Package entry points (`exports`): `vantaui-css` (min css), `vantaui-css/css` (unminified), `vantaui-css/min`, `vantaui-css/stats` (VantaStats add-on), `vantaui-css/prose` (VantaProse add-on), `vantaui-css/js`, `vantaui-css/js/global`, `vantaui-css/vue`, `vantaui-css/nuxt`, `vantaui-css/src/*`. Add-ons load separately so core stays slim. See the Extensions section. **Breaking in v2.0:** `vui-prose` moved out of core into the `vantaui-css/prose` add-on (same markup, new import).

## Mental model: Settings, Elements, and Helpers (the BeerCSS way)

- **Settings** = design tokens (CSS custom properties). Override any, anywhere because they cascade.
- **Elements** = semantic HTML styled directly inside `.vui`. No component classes.
- **Helpers** = one short element-scoped word to change a default (e.g. `<button class="danger">`).

State is semantic first: current nav item = `aria-current="page"` (or alias `class="active"`); invalid field = `aria-invalid`; a switch = `role="switch"`; alerts = `role="status"` / `role="alert"`; tabs = `role="tablist"`/`role="tab"`/`role="tabpanel"`.

### Minimal example

```html
<body class="vui">
  <header>
    <a href="/">VANTA<b>UI</b></a>
    <nav><a aria-current="page">Overview</a><a>Units</a></nav>
    <menu><button class="icon"><i>settings</i></button></menu>
  </header>
  <main>
    <h1>Command Center</h1>
    <article>
      <header><small class="vui-eyebrow">Diagnostics</small></header>
      <p>A chamfered panel. The header drew its own divider.</p>
      <footer><button type="submit">Authorize</button></footer>
    </article>
    <button>Override</button>
    <button class="danger">Abort</button>
    <label><input type="checkbox" role="switch" checked /> Active camo</label>
    <meter min="0" max="100" low="30" high="70" optimum="100" value="86"></meter>
  </main>
</body>
```

### What bare HTML gives you (inside `.vui`, no classes)

| Write | You get |
| --- | --- |
| `<h1>`…`<h6>` | Uppercase, tracked display headings |
| `<button>` | Chamfered outline button |
| `<button type="submit">` | Filled primary button |
| `<button class="icon"><i>close</i></button>` | Square icon button |
| `<i>home</i>` | The "home" Material Symbols glyph |
| `<a> <code> <kbd> <mark> <pre> <blockquote> <hr>` | Tactical inline + block styles |
| `<article>` | Chamfered panel; its `<header>`/`<footer>` auto-divide |
| `<input> <textarea> <select>` | Inset terminal fields with focus glow |
| `<input type="checkbox\|radio">` | Notched / round emissive marks |
| `<input type="checkbox" role="switch">` | Angular toggle switch |
| `<input type="range">` | Tactical slider |
| `<meter>` / `<progress>` | Telemetry bars (`<meter>` colors by `low`/`high`/`optimum`) |
| `<table>` | Mono data grid, eyebrow headers, cyan row hover |
| `<dl>` | Label │ value telemetry readout |
| `<details><summary>` | Zero-JS chamfered disclosure |
| `<dialog>` | Chamfered modal with scrim backdrop |
| `<header>` containing a `<nav>` | App bar; collapses to a burger when narrow |
| `<footer>` | Footer bar (`status` / `columns` shapes) |
| `<nav><ol>…</ol></nav>` | Breadcrumb (auto-detected, zero classes) |
| `.vui` element directly holding a `<main>` | Full app frame (landmarks placed by element) |
| `[role="status"]` / `[role="alert"]` | Info / threat alert with accent rail + glyph |
| `[role="tablist"]` / `[role="tab"]` | Tab strip (state via the JS helper or your framework) |

---

# Layout & Utilities

Free-floating helpers here use the `vui-` prefix.

## Breakpoints (mobile-first, min-width)

**s** 36rem/576px · **m** 48rem/768px · **l** 64rem/1024px · **xl** 80rem/1280px. Tokens: `--bp-{s,m,l,xl}`.

## Container

- `vui-container`: centered, max-width `--container-max`, inline padding `--container-pad`.
- `vui-container--narrow`: max-width `--container-narrow`. · `vui-container--fluid`: has no max-width.

## Content section (`vui-section`)

A titled content band: leading hairline divider, generous block padding, and a scroll anchor (the first stacked section drops its divider). Sets no `display`, so compose with `vui-prose` (measure) or `vui-stack` (rhythm). A child heading is the title; `<p class="blurb">` is the muted lead.

```html
<section class="vui-section vui-prose" id="philosophy">
  <h2>Philosophy</h2>
  <p class="blurb">One short framing sentence.</p>
</section>
```

## Named flex composites (prefer these)

`vui-row` (+ `vui-row--wrap`) · `vui-col` · `vui-stack` (vertical rhythm, gap `--space-5`) · `vui-cluster` (wrapping horizontal) · `vui-center` (grid place-items center) · `vui-between` (space-between row) · `vui-spacer` (`flex:1 1 auto`).

## Auto grid

`vui-autogrid` (+ `vui-autogrid--fit`); control min track with `style="--vui-min: 16rem"`.

## 12-column grid

`vui-grid` wrapping children with span classes `vui-s1…vui-s12`, `vui-m1…vui-m12`, `vui-l1…vui-l12`. Children stack full-width on mobile, opt into spans at each breakpoint up.

```html
<div class="vui-grid">
  <article class="vui-s12 vui-m6 vui-l4">…</article>
  <article class="vui-s12 vui-m6 vui-l8">…</article>
</div>
<div class="vui-autogrid" style="--vui-min: 16rem">… cards …</div>
```

## Show / hide (hide-only, preserves natural display)

`vui-until-{s,m,l,xl}` (hide at/above the bp) · `vui-from-{s,m,l,xl}` (hide below the bp).

## Utility classes (all `vui-` prefixed)

Spacing index N → `--space-N`. Scale: 0=0, 1=2px, 2=4px, 3=8px, 4=12px, 5=16px, 6=20px, 7=24px, 8=32px (utilities cover 0–8; tokens go to 13).

- **Display**: `vui-block` `vui-inline-block` `vui-inline` `vui-flex` `vui-inline-flex` `vui-grid-d` `vui-contents` `vui-hidden` `vui-sr-only`.
- **Flex/grid behaviour**: `vui-flex-row` `vui-flex-col` `vui-wrap` `vui-nowrap` `vui-flex-1` `vui-grow` `vui-shrink-0`; `vui-items-{start,center,end,stretch,baseline}`; `vui-justify-{start,center,end,between,around}`.
- **Position/sizing**: `vui-relative` `vui-absolute` `vui-sticky` `vui-w-full` `vui-h-full` `vui-w-auto` `vui-min-w-0` `vui-overflow-auto` `vui-overflow-hidden`.
- **Gap**: `vui-gap-0…vui-gap-8`.
- **Padding**: `vui-p-0…8` (all), `vui-pi-{0,2..8}` (inline), `vui-pb-{0,2..8}` (block).
- **Margin**: `vui-m-{0,2,3,4,5,6}` `vui-m-auto` `vui-mi-auto`; `vui-mb-{0,2..8}` (block); `vui-mbe-{3,4,5,6}` (block-end only).
- **Text**: `vui-text-{start,center,end}` `vui-upper` `vui-nowrap-text` `vui-truncate`.
- **Font family**: `vui-font-{display,ui,hud,mono}`. **Weight**: `vui-fw-{light,regular,medium,semibold,bold}`. **Tracking**: `vui-tracked` `vui-tracked-wider` `vui-tracked-widest`. **Size**: `vui-text-{2xs,xs,sm,base,md,lg,xl,2xl,3xl,4xl,5xl}`.
- **Text color**: `vui-text-{primary,secondary,muted,faint,cyan,amber,red,green}`.
- **Surfaces**: `vui-bg-{void,base,raised,panel,inset}`.
- **Borders**: `vui-border` `vui-border-strong` `vui-border-accent` `vui-border-0`.
- **Glows**: `vui-glow-{cyan,amber,red,green}` `vui-text-glow`.
- **Misc**: `vui-pointer` `vui-select-none` `vui-flicker` `vui-scanning`.

## HUD primitives (base building blocks, `vui-` prefixed)

- **Type roles**: `vui-display` `vui-title` `vui-heading` `vui-mono` `vui-readout` (big numeric) `vui-label` `vui-eyebrow` (renders a `//` prefix) `vui-signal` (glowing cyan text).
- **Plates / frames**: `vui-chamfer` (cut top-left + bottom-right), `vui-notch` (all four corners cut), `vui-brackets` (L-shaped corner ticks), `vui-rule` (hairline with fading center). `vui-chamfer`/`vui-notch` clip paint AND contain inline overflow.
- **Panel surface** (`vui-panel`): full panel look (background, border, shadow, chamfer/corner-shape, and `overflow-x: clip`) on any element. Use this when `<article>` is not possible, such as when using `<router-link>` which renders as `<a>`. Tones like `cyan`, `amber`, `red`, and `green` remap `--_c`. Add `linked` for hover interaction (shifting the border and background). Add `vui-clip` to keep the open unstroked diagonal, and compose it with any layout utility.
- **Textured backdrops**: `vui-bg-grid` `vui-bg-scan` `vui-bg-hud` (ink + grid + scanlines + vignette).
- **Status dot**: `vui-dot` (default online/green, pulsing) + `vui-dot--warn` `vui-dot--threat` `vui-dot--cyan` `vui-dot--idle`.
- **Labelled divider**: `<div class="divider" data-label="SECTOR">` (label between fading hairlines; drop `data-label` for a plain divider).

## Prose layout (`vui-prose`): moved to an add-on in v2.0

The prose layout left the core bundle in v2.0 and is now the VantaProse add-on (`import 'vantaui-css/prose'`). Full reference is in the Extensions section below.

---

# Navigation & Chrome

State is semantic-first: current item = `aria-current="page"` (alias `.active`); a leading `<i>` is its icon.

## App frame ("write the landmarks, get the layout")

Any `.vui` element that directly holds a `<main>` becomes an app frame and places landmarks by element, without requiring wrapper classes. On `<body>` it fills the viewport (`100dvh`); nested in a sized box it fills the box.

```html
<body class="vui">
  <header>…</header>            <!-- top bar -->
  <nav class="left">…</nav>      <!-- side rail: vertical ≥768px, bottom bar below -->
  <main>…</main>                <!-- the scrolling stage -->
  <footer>…</footer>            <!-- bottom bar (or use <nav class="bottom">) -->
</body>
```

- A bare `<aside>` works as a left rail; `<aside class="right">` / `<nav class="right">` is a right rail. Both rails at once are supported.
- A drawer sidebar (`<nav class="left drawer">` / `<aside class="drawer">`) sizes the rail column to `--drawer-width` (default 16.5rem).
- `header.inset` flips the default: rail(s) rise full-height, the header is confined beside them.
- The stage shows a faint hex-grid backdrop by default. Opt into vignette + scanlines with `.vignette` on the frame or `<body>` (off by default).
- A `<footer>` and a bottom command bar (a side rail dropped to the floor, or `<nav class="bottom">`) can coexist: on phones they stack in separate rows — footer just above the command bar — instead of overlapping. From the `m` breakpoint up the rail returns to a column.
- Mobile viewport hardening: the frame and `<body>` are both sized in `dvh` (not `vh`) so the page never overhangs the visible viewport while the toolbar is shown (which would make the document scrollable and let the bottom command bar scroll off after an Android pull-to-refresh). The stage and body set `overscroll-behavior: contain` so a pull-to-refresh / rubber-band can't displace the fixed shell.
- Overflow philosophy: `<main>` is the scroll container; `min-inline-size: 0` confines wide children so chrome never slides. Genuine misuse scrolls visibly rather than being silently clipped.

## Header / app bar

A `<header>` becomes a tactical app bar when it carries a `<nav>`, is the top bar of an app frame, or sits directly on a `.vui` `<body>`.

```html
<header>
  <a href="/">VANTA<b>UI</b></a>           <!-- brand: first link/heading; <b> is accent-lit; an <img>/<svg> is the emblem -->
  <nav>                                     <!-- inline primary nav; first <nav> pushes everything after it right -->
    <a aria-current="page">Overview</a>
    <a>Units <small>12</small></a>          <!-- trailing <small> = count chip -->
  </nav>
  <form><i>search</i><input type="search" placeholder="Search…"></form>  <!-- inline search -->
  <menu><button class="icon"><i>settings</i></button></menu>             <!-- trailing actions -->
  <button class="icon" aria-controls="drawer" data-open="drawer"><i>menu</i></button>  <!-- burger -->
</header>
```

The header is a **container-query context**: when the BAR itself narrows below 52rem, the inline nav + search collapse and the menu button (any header `<button>` with `aria-controls`/`commandfor`/`command`/`data-open`) appears. Inside it: an `<hr>` is a vertical divider; a `<menu>` is the trailing actions cluster. Shape/tone helpers: `sticky` `glow` `bare` `tall` `center` `float`. Alignment: `max` · `right` / `left`.

## Footer

- **default**: a hairline strip featuring the brand, a `<nav>` of links, and a `<small>` legal line. Use `max` or `right` for alignment.
- **`footer.status`**: a dense HUD telemetry bar where each direct child is a readout cell. Use `.signal` to light a cell, `.max` to push cells apart, and `<b>` for the value.
- **`footer.columns`**: a sitemap where columns are `<nav>`, `<section>`, or `<div>` blocks with a heading (`h4` to `h6` or `.label`) and links. A child marked `.bottom` spans full width.
- Tone: `glow` · `base`.

## Navigation rails, bottom bar, drawers

- **Side rail**: `<nav class="left">` / `<nav class="right">` / a bare `<aside>`. Bottom bar on phones, vertical column from `m` up. A leading `<img>`/`<svg>` is an emblem; `.max` a spacer.
- **Bottom command bar**: `<nav class="bottom">`; pin a standalone one with `nav.bottom.fixed`.
- **Off-canvas drawer**: `<dialog class="left">` or `<dialog class="right">`. This is a native `<dialog>` element that is animated and opened via `showModal()`. The JS `drawers()` helper automatically wires elements with `[data-open]`, `[data-close]`, and backdrop dismissal. Structure: `header`, `nav` (for the link stack), and `footer`.
- **Persistent drawer sidebar**: `<aside class="drawer">` or `<nav class="drawer">`. This acts as a full-height column on desktop and collapses to an off-canvas layout on mobile (toggled via `.active` or `.open`). Adding `.right` flips the drawer to the trailing side, and the width is controlled via `--drawer-width`.

```html
<dialog class="left" id="drawer">
  <header><a href="/"><img src="mark.svg" alt=""> VANTAUI</a><button class="icon" data-close><i>close</i></button></header>
  <nav>
    <h6>Sectors</h6>
    <a aria-current="page"><i>dashboard</i> Overview</a>
    <a><i>group</i> Units</a>
  </nav>
  <footer><button class="block" data-close>Close</button></footer>
</dialog>
```

## Breadcrumb (detected, zero classes)

A `<nav>` directly containing an `<ol>` (or `ol.breadcrumb`). `/` separators inserted automatically; mark the last item `aria-current="page"`.

```html
<nav><ol><li><a>Home</a></li><li><a>Units</a></li><li aria-current="page">Alpha</li></ol></nav>
```

## Dropdown / action menu

- **`<details class="dropdown">`**: a zero-JS solution that works everywhere. The `<summary>` element acts as the trigger, and the panel is a `<menu>`, `<nav>`, or `<ul>`. The JS `menus()` helper adds click-outside and Esc key dismissal, upgrading the panel to the Popover top layer where supported.
- **`<button popovertarget>` + `[popover].menu`**: a modern top-layer option with light-dismiss.

Items are `<a>`/`<button>` (lead with an `<i>`); an `<hr>` divides, an `<h6>`/`.label` is a group heading, `.danger` tones an item red. Add `.right` to align the panel to the trailing edge. A trailing `<i>` in the summary is a chevron that rotates open.

```html
<details class="dropdown">
  <summary>Actions <i>expand_more</i></summary>
  <menu>
    <h6>Unit</h6>
    <button><i>edit</i> Rename</button>
    <hr>
    <button class="danger"><i>delete</i> Decommission</button>
  </menu>
</details>
```

## Tabs

A `[role="tablist"]` of `[role="tab"]` buttons; active tab read from `aria-selected="true"` (or `.active`). Panels are `[role="tabpanel"]`, toggled with `[hidden]`. A trailing `<small>` is a count chip. Wire with the JS `tabs()` helper or your framework.

```html
<div role="tablist">
  <button role="tab" aria-selected="true" aria-controls="p1">Sensors</button>
  <button role="tab" aria-controls="p2">Comms <small>3</small></button>
</div>
<div role="tabpanel" id="p1">…</div>
<div role="tabpanel" id="p2" hidden>…</div>
```

## Pagination

`<nav class="pagination">` wrapping a `<ul>`/`<ol>` of page links (`<a>`/`<button>`; a lone `<i>` is an icon prev/next). Mark the current page `aria-current="page"`, unavailable controls `disabled`/`aria-disabled`. A bare `<span>` is an ellipsis gap. `.compact` tightens it.

## Toolbar

A `<div role="toolbar">` (or `.toolbar`) is a chamfered control track holding buttons, button groups (`<menu class="group">`), segmented controls, an `<hr>` separator, a `.max` spacer, and short `<span>`/`<small>` labels. JS `toolbars()` gives it roving-tabindex keyboard nav. `.vertical` stands it on end.

---

# Forms & Buttons

## Buttons

A bare `<button>` is a chamfered outline control; `type="submit"` auto-promotes to filled primary. Style other elements as a button with `.button`. Tune with one word:

- **Variant**: `fill` · `ghost`. **Tones**: `amber` · `danger` (red) · `secure` (green); default cyan. **Sizes**: `small` · `large`. **Width**: `block`.
- **Icon-only**: `.icon` → 38px square (also `icon small` 30px, `icon large` 46px). Icon + text just works without `.icon`. `aria-pressed="true"` lights an icon toggle.

```html
<button>Override</button>
<button type="submit">Authorize</button>
<button class="danger">Abort</button>
<button><i>save</i> Save</button>
<button class="icon"><i>close</i></button>
<a class="button ghost" href="/docs">Docs</a>
```

**Button group**: `<menu class="group">` (or any `.group`) is an inline-flex cluster of buttons with consistent gaps.

## Text fields, textarea, select

`<input>` (text/email/password/number/search/url/…), `<textarea>`, `<select>` become inset terminal fields (mono, gentle chamfer, focus glow), full-width in their container. `<textarea>` resizes vertically; `<select>` gets a cyan chevron. Focus → accent border + glow. Validation: `aria-invalid="true"` → red border + glow.

## Field wrapper

A `<label>` wrapping a text control stacks: leading `<span>` = uppercase label, control below, trailing `<small>` = hint (`<small class="error">` is red). A `<label>` wrapping a checkbox/radio lays out as an inline row.

```html
<label>
  <span>Callsign</span>
  <input type="text" placeholder="e.g. RAVEN-1" />
  <small>Letters, numbers, dash.</small>
</label>
<label><input type="checkbox" /> Enable telemetry</label>
```

## Checkbox, radio, switch, range

- `<input type="checkbox">` Notch mark and `<input type="radio">` round mark: both are drawn directly on the element and light up in cyan when checked.
- `<input type="checkbox" role="switch">`: an angular toggle switch.
- `<input type="range">`: a tactical slider with a chamfered cyan thumb.

```html
<label><input type="checkbox" role="switch" checked /> Active camo</label>
<label><span>Output</span><input type="range" min="0" max="100" value="72" /></label>
```

Any native control not fully restyled inherits `accent-color: var(--accent)`.

## Combobox / autocomplete

A text `<input>` combined with a `<ul popover="manual" class="listbox" role="listbox">` of `<li role="option">` rows. The list renders in the top layer, meaning it is never clipped by an ancestor panel's chamfer/notch `clip-path` or `overflow`. No body teleport is needed because the markup remains in place. Highlight the active row with `.active` (or `[aria-selected="true"]`). Position the list under the field using `VantaUI.placePopover(list, input, { matchWidth: true })` on open, and call it again on scroll or resize. You manage filtering, keyboard navigation, and the `showPopover()` or `hidePopover()` actions. This requires the Popover API (Baseline 2024).

```html
<label><span>Names</span>
  <input role="combobox" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="names-ac" autocomplete="off" />
</label>
<ul id="names-ac" popover="manual" class="listbox" role="listbox">
  <li role="option" class="active">Ada Lovelace</li>
  <li role="option">Alan Turing</li>
</ul>
```

---

# Data Display

## Panel (`<article>`)

A bare `<article>` is a chamfered plate; an inner `<header>`/`<footer>` auto-divides. Depth: `raised` · `inset` · `flat`. Stage: `stage` recesses the body into a dark ink+scanlines viewport while header/footer rise to the panel surface and frame it (framed-console / readout look). Shape: `notch` · `brackets`. Glow: `glow`. Color tint: `cyan` · `amber` · `red` · `green`. Tint intensity: `dim` · `vivid`. Linked/tappable: `linked` (hover shifts border to accent, raises background). Combine freely (`"amber glow"`, `"green raised"`, `"cyan linked"`).

```html
<article class="raised">
  <header><small class="vui-eyebrow">Diagnostics</small> <small>v2.1</small></header>
  <p>A chamfered panel; the header drew its own divider.</p>
  <footer><button type="submit">Authorize</button></footer>
</article>
```

(`<article class="vui-prose">` opts out of panel styling into the prose layout.)

## Table

A bare `<table>` is a mono data grid (eyebrow headers, cyan row hover). Use real `<thead>`/`<tbody>`/`<th>`/`<td>`; wide tables self-scroll.

## Description list, key/value, stat

- `<dl>`: a label-value telemetry grid (`<dt>` label, `<dd>` monospaced value); add `.stacked` for label-over-value.
- `.kv`: a single key/value row (label on the left, monospaced value on the right).
- `.stat`: a large numeric readout where `<b>` is the number and `<span>` is the caption. Use `.stat.signal` or `.stat.amber` to recolor.

```html
<dl><dt>Callsign</dt><dd>RAVEN-1</dd><dt>Sector</dt><dd>NE-04</dd></dl>
<div class="stat signal"><b>98.6</b><span>Integrity</span></div>
```

## Badge

`.badge` chip (on `<span>`/`<a>`/`<mark>`). Tone: `cyan` · `amber` · `red` · `green` · `neutral`. `.solid` fills, `.dot` adds a live dot. Long labels truncate.

```html
<span class="badge green dot">Online</span>
<span class="badge red solid">Threat</span>
```

## Avatar

Initials on `<span>`/`<div>` or an `<img>`. Shape: `round` · `hex`. Size: `small` · `large`. Status frame: `online` · `busy` · `idle` · `offline`. `.avatar-group` overlaps several.

```html
<span class="avatar online">R1</span>
<img class="avatar round large" src="op.jpg" alt="Raven-1" />
<div class="avatar-group"><span class="avatar">R1</span><span class="avatar">B2</span></div>
```

## Timeline

`<ol class="timeline">`: a vertical event feed where each `<li>` is a glowing node on a rail. Lead with a `<time>` stamp, and tone a node with a color helper directly on the `<li>`. Add `.log` for a dense, monospaced feed.

```html
<ol class="timeline">
  <li class="green"><time>09:42:01</time> Uplink established.</li>
  <li class="red"><time>09:45:00</time> Link lost.</li>
</ol>
```

## Stepper

`<ol class="stepper">`: numbered steps joined by a connector. Mark the active step with `aria-current="step"`, and add `.done` to a completed step to replace its number with a checkmark. You can also add `.vertical` for a vertical layout. Since it is static, you must wire the navigation logic yourself.

```html
<ol class="stepper">
  <li class="done">Authenticate</li>
  <li aria-current="step">Configure</li>
  <li>Deploy</li>
</ol>
```

## Tree

`<ul class="tree">`: a hierarchy with guide rails. A branch is structured as `<li><details><summary>…</summary><ul>…</ul></details></li>` (collapsing with zero JavaScript). A leaf is a plain `<li><a>` or `<li><span>`. Lead rows with an `<i>` folder/file icon, and mark the selected leaf with `aria-current="page"`. For a full ARIA tree widget, add `role="tree"`, `treeitem`, or `group` with `aria-expanded`, and manage focus in JS.

```html
<ul class="tree">
  <li><details open>
    <summary><i>folder</i> Sector NE</summary>
    <ul><li><a aria-current="page"><i>description</i> Brief.md</a></li></ul>
  </details></li>
</ul>
```

## Carousel

`<div class="carousel">`: a horizontal scroll-snap track where the direct children are the slides. It supports swipe, scroll, and keyboard navigation. On native CSS-carousel browsers (Chrome 135+) arrows and a dot row are added automatically with no JavaScript. On browsers without it (Firefox and Safari today), the optional `vantaui-css/js` (`carousels()`) injects matching arrows and dots; without that script the track still scrolls, swipes, and takes keyboard focus. Size slides using `--vui-slide`, and customize with `.peek`, `.full`, `.clean`, `.no-arrows`, or `.no-dots`.

```html
<div class="carousel peek" style="--vui-slide: 18rem">
  <article>Slide 1</article><article>Slide 2</article>
</div>
```

## Rating

`<fieldset class="rating">`: a row of pips where each is a `<label>` with a leading `<i>` immediately followed by its radio `<input>`, authored 1 to 5. The `<legend>` is the group label. Tone the control using a color word, and add `.readonly` for a static readout. This component requires no JavaScript.

## Segmented control

`.segmented` track of `<label>` chips, each wrapping a radio `<input>` (+ optional leading `<i>`). The checked segment lights. `.small`, plus color tones. Zero-JS radiogroup.

```html
<div class="segmented">
  <label><input type="radio" name="view" checked /> Map</label>
  <label><input type="radio" name="view" /> List</label>
</div>
```

---

# Feedback & Status

## Alerts (inline)

`[role="status"]` = info (cyan); `[role="alert"]` = threat (red). Accent rail + leading glyph; an optional `<strong>`/`<h*>` is the title: it stacks above the body on a narrow alert and goes inline once wide enough (container query on the alert's own width). Re-tone: `info` · `warn` · `threat` · `secure`. Override the glyph with `--vui-alert-glyph`.

```html
<div role="status"><strong>Online</strong> Telemetry uplink restored.</div>
<div role="alert"><strong>Threat</strong> Hostile contact, sector NE-04.</div>
```

## Toast (transient)

Raise imperatively with the JS `toast()` helper (owns an `aria-live` `.toaster` region, reuses the alert rail + glyph). Tone: `info` · `warn` · `threat` · `secure`. Place the region with `.start`/`.top`/`.center`.

```js
import { toast } from 'vantaui-css/js';
toast('Telemetry uplink restored', { tone: 'secure', title: 'ONLINE' });
```

## Meters & progress

- Native `<progress>`: a cyan telemetry bar. Native `<meter>`: colors itself automatically by `low`, `high`, and `optimum` thresholds.
- `.meter` helper: a labelled accent bar with no JavaScript. It consists of a `<span>` or `<label>` and a `<b>` value (leave empty to render `--value%` via a CSS counter). Tone with `cyan`, `amber`, `red`, or `green`, use `.segmented` for ticks, and set `--value` (and `--max`).

```html
<meter min="0" max="100" low="30" high="70" optimum="100" value="86"></meter>
<div class="meter amber" style="--value:72"><span>Power</span><b></b></div>
```

## Radial gauge

`.gauge`: a 270-degree arc in pure CSS driven by `--value`. The structure is a `<b>` tag for the number (leave it empty to use a CSS counter), an optional `<small>` unit, and a `<span>` caption. Fluid by default: it scales with the viewport (clamped ~6rem–11rem), ring thickness included; set `--size` for a fixed footprint and `--thickness` for a thinner/heavier ring. Tone with color helpers.

```html
<div class="gauge" style="--value:72"><b></b><small>%</small><span>Integrity</span></div>
<div class="gauge red" style="--value:18; --size:7rem"><b></b><small>%</small><span>Fuel</span></div>
```

## Spinner (indeterminate)

`.spinner`: a sweeping ring on a `<span>` or `<div>`. Tone using color helper words, and size with `small` or `large`. A trailing text node functions as an inline label, and `.block` centers it. Mark with `role="status"` and `aria-label`.

```html
<span class="spinner" role="status" aria-label="Loading"></span>
<span class="spinner cyan">Syncing…</span>
```

## Skeleton

`.skeleton`: a dim scanning placeholder. Options include a bare block, `.text` for a type line (add `.last` for a short final line), `.circle` for a disc, and `.block` for a tall block. Mark the group with `aria-hidden="true"`.

## Empty state

`.empty`: a centered placeholder consisting of a leading `<i>` glyph, a heading, a `<p>`, and a trailing `<menu>` or `.actions` for action buttons.

```html
<div class="empty">
  <i>satellite_alt</i><h3>No active units</h3>
  <p>Deploy a unit to begin receiving telemetry.</p>
  <menu><button type="submit">Deploy unit</button></menu>
</div>
```

## Live status dot

`vui-dot` (pulsing online/green) + `vui-dot--warn` · `vui-dot--threat` · `vui-dot--cyan` · `vui-dot--idle`.

---

# Theming & Design Tokens

Tokens live on `:root` and are available everywhere (outside `.vui`, only tokens are defined). Override any token anywhere because they cascade. The single signal accent is electric cyan (`--accent`).

```css
:root {
  --accent: oklch(84.6% 0.133 212.1);
  --bevel-md: 14px;
  --container-max: 90rem;
}
```

## Colors (OKLCH)

Every `src/` token ships twice (sRGB hex fallback, then `oklch()`). Palette meaning: cyan = you/system, amber = caution/hostile, red = threat, green = secure/online.

- **Scales**: `--ink-{1000,900,800,700,600,500,400}`, `--cyan-{50,200,400,500,600,700}`+`--cyan-glow`, `--amber-{300,400,500,600}`+`--amber-glow`, `--red-{400,500,600}`+`--red-glow`, `--green-{400,500,600}`+`--green-glow`, `--slate-{50,200,300,400,500,600}`.
- **Aliases**: `--bg-{void,base,raised}`, `--surface-{panel,panel-2,inset}`, `--text-{primary,secondary,muted,faint,signal,invert}`, `--accent` `--accent-strong` `--accent-dim` `--accent-glow`, `--status-{online,warn,threat,hostile}`, `--hairline` `--hairline-strong` `--hairline-accent` `--divider` `--scrim`.

## Spacing & geometry

`--space-0…--space-13`, `--radius-{none,xs,sm,pill}`, `--bevel-{sm,md,lg,xl}`, `--clip-chamfer` / `--clip-notch`, `--border-{hair,thin,bold}`, `--container-max`, `--container-narrow`, `--container-pad`, `--gutter`, `--hud-edge`, `--drawer-width`, and `--bp-{s,m,l,xl}`. Every beveled component upgrades its chamfer/notch from `clip-path` to `border-radius` and `corner-shape: bevel` where supported (Chromium 139+): `<article>`/`.vui-panel`, `.vui-chamfer`/`.vui-notch`, `<pre>`, buttons, badges, inputs/select/textarea/checkbox, tables, menus & dropdowns, dialogs & tooltips, tabs, alerts, toasts, avatars, toolbars, segmented controls, pagination, steppers, skeletons, and carousel controls. This offers the same look, but shapes only the box so overflowing text or dropdown menus are never clipped. Elsewhere (Firefox/Safari as of 2026), `clip-path` remains the fallback. The `.vui-clip` class forces the clip-path engine, preserving the open, unstroked diagonal corner, though its content will be clipped (which you must manage yourself). It works on a single element (opts just that element out) or on a root/ancestor: `<body class="vui vui-clip">` flips the whole subtree to clip-path, so one class switches the entire UI between the two engines. One-sided diagonals (drawer trailing edge, meter/progress bars, range/switch thumbs) are not corner bevels and stay on clip-path everywhere.

## Typography

`--font-{display,ui,hud,mono,icon}`, `--fw-{light,regular,medium,semibold,bold}`, fluid `--fs-{2xs,xs,sm,base,md,lg,xl,2xl,3xl,4xl,5xl}` (base+ use `clamp()`), `--lh-{tight,snug,normal,mono}`, `--ls-{tight,normal,wide,wider,widest}`, semantic `--text-{display,title,heading,body,label,mono}-size`.

## Effects & motion

`--glow-cyan-{sm,md,lg}` `--glow-{amber,red,green}-md`, `--text-glow-{cyan,amber}`, `--shadow-{panel,raised,inset}`, textures `--scanlines` `--grid-fine`(+`--grid-fine-size`) `--vignette`, motion `--ease-{hud,snap,inout}` `--dur-{fast,base,slow,boot}`, `--focus-ring`.

Keyframes: `vui-flicker`, `vui-scan`, `vui-pulse`, `vui-sweep`. A global `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` rule neutralizes ambient animation.

## Fonts loading

Fonts (Chakra Petch, Rajdhani, Share Tech Mono, and Material Symbols) load via a remote `@import` in the stylesheet for convenience, but this is not required. **Self-hosting is recommended for production** because it drops the extra connection to `fonts.googleapis.com` and the render-blocking remote `@import`. It also keeps requests off Google's CDN since fonts are served from your own origin. If your framework has a font manager (such as [Nuxt Fonts](https://fonts.nuxt.com/) or [`@nuxtjs/google-fonts`](https://google-fonts.nuxtjs.org/)), let it download and self-host the four families automatically so you can remove the remote `@import`. Without one: open each family on Google Fonts ([Chakra Petch](https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Chakra+Petch), [Rajdhani](https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Rajdhani), [Share Tech Mono](https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Share+Tech+Mono), and [Material Symbols](https://fonts.google.com/icons)), download the files, serve them from your own origin, vendor a copy of the VantaUI CSS with the font `@import` line removed, and add local `@font-face` rules. Token family names (`--font-display` etc.) are unchanged, so nothing else moves. The first three are licensed under SIL OFL 1.1, while Material Symbols is licensed under Apache 2.0. When self-hosting these files, please include those license texts alongside them.

---

# Optional JavaScript (`vantaui-css/js`)

ES module exports (also attached to `window.vui` as a plain script; auto-inits on `DOMContentLoaded`). Every function takes an optional `root` (default `document`) and is idempotent.

### When is JS required?

The CSS is fully standalone. The behaviours module layers interactivity on top; some things need it and others are only enhanced by it:

**Always needs the script (no CSS path exists)**
- **Tabs:** without the script, tabs are just styled buttons with no panel switching or keyboard navigation.
- **Toast:** programmatic only, no CSS equivalent.
- **Live clock:** `[data-vui-clock]` stays empty.
- **Animated meters / gauges:** `data-animate` is a no-op; `--value` stays static.

**Needs the script on Firefox and Safari (Chrome handles it natively)**
- **Carousel:** Chrome 135+ renders prev/next buttons and a dot row natively via the CSS carousel API. Firefox and Safari do not. Without `carousels()`, the strip scrolls and accepts keyboard focus but has no visible controls.

**Enhanced by the script; a working CSS fallback applies**
- **Dropdown menus:** without `menus()`, `<details class="dropdown">` still opens natively, but the panel is in-flow and may be clipped by an ancestor `overflow` / `clip-path`, with no Popover light-dismiss, Esc, or scroll-pinning.
- **Drawers / dialogs:** without `drawers()`, wire open/close with `onclick="…showModal()"` inline; all visual and animation CSS still applies.
- **Tooltips:** without `tooltips()`, `[data-tip]` gets a CSS `::after` fallback with no viewport clamping or scroll-tracking.
- **Toolbar keyboard:** without `toolbars()`, arrow-key navigation does not work and standard tab order applies.

- **`init(root = document)`**: runs all behaviours below.
- **`tabs(root)`**: wires `[role="tablist"]`/`[role="tab"]` (click and arrow keys). Panels are matched by `aria-controls` or `data-tab`, and it toggles `[hidden]` while setting `aria-selected` and `tabindex`.
- **`setValue(el, n, duration = 600)`**: animates a `.meter` or `.gauge` `--value` using an ease-out cubic curve (respecting reduced motion). The maximum is read from `--max` or `data-max` (defaulting to 100).
- Auto-animate (via `init`): elements with `data-value` + `data-animate` fill from 0 on scroll into view.
- **`drawers(root)`**: registers click handlers where `[data-open="id"]` opens a `<dialog id>` using `showModal()` (or toggles `.active` or `.open` on non-dialog drawers), `[data-close]` closes the nearest drawer, a backdrop click dismisses a dialog (which you can disable with `data-no-dismiss`), and links inside drawers automatically close them when clicked.
- **`menus(root)`**: handles `<details class="dropdown">` dropdowns. Where the Popover API is available, it upgrades the panel to the top layer (supporting light-dismiss, Esc key dismissal, and exclusive opening), positioning it below the trigger (adding `.right` to right-align). The open panel stays pinned to its trigger on scroll/resize (via `trackAnchor`). It follows the page and never closes on scroll. The no-Popover fallback panel is in-flow `position: absolute`, so it already moves with the page.
- **`placePopover(panel, anchor, opts?)`**: positions a top-layer `[popover]` under an anchor off its viewport rectangle (called after `showPopover()` and again on scroll or resize, see `trackAnchor`). Options include `{ align = 'start' | 'end', gap = 6, matchWidth = false }`. This is the primitive behind `menus()`, useful for building a `<ul popover class="listbox">` autocomplete component that escapes ancestor `clip-path` constraints.
- **`trackAnchor(reposition, isOpen)`**: keeps a floating panel pinned to its anchor across `scroll` (captured + passive) and `resize`, re-running `reposition()` while `isOpen()` holds. Scrolling repositions, never dismisses: the mechanism behind dropdowns, tooltips, and comboboxes following the page instead of drifting or snapping shut. Returns `stop()` to detach. (Native `<select>` popups are OS-drawn and may still close on scroll, outside library control.)
- **`toolbars(root)`**: roving tabindex for `[role="toolbar"]` (with `.vertical` enabling up/down arrow navigation).
- **`carousels(root)`**: graceful fallback controls for `.carousel`. No-op where the native CSS carousel exists (Chrome 135+ draws arrows + dots itself). Where it does not (Firefox/Safari today), it wraps the track in `.vui-carousel-shell` and injects matching prev/next buttons and a dot per slide, wired to scroll and reflect the current slide (arrows disable at the ends, `prefers-reduced-motion` respected, honours `.no-arrows` / `.no-dots`, idempotent). Detection is `CSS.supports('scroll-marker-group: after')`. The strip stays scroll/swipe/keyboard navigable even without the script.
- **`tooltips(root)`**: renders `[data-tip="text"]` on hover or focus using a body-level fixed element to prevent clipping; it follows the anchor across scroll/resize (via `trackAnchor`), clamps within the viewport and flips below the anchor when there isn't room above, and only hides on leave/blur, not on scroll.
- Live clock (via `init`): writes `HH:MM:SS` into `[data-vui-clock]` every second.
- **`toast(message, options?)`**: programmatic snackbar. Options include `{ tone, title, duration = 4000, role = 'status', dismissible = true, placement }`. The `tone` option accepts a bare string shorthand.

```js
import { init, toast, setValue } from 'vantaui-css/js';
init();
toast('Telemetry uplink restored', { tone: 'secure', title: 'ONLINE' });
```

Default export: `VantaUI = { init, tabs, setValue, drawers, tooltips, menus, placePopover, trackAnchor, toolbars, carousels, toast }`. Entry points: `vantaui-css/js` (ESM), `vantaui-css/js/global` (plain-script global).

### Scrollspy (active nav link)

Mark the sidebar nav link for the currently visible section using `IntersectionObserver`. This is how the VantaUI docs sync their `<aside class="drawer">` nav. No library export needed.

```js
const links = [...document.querySelectorAll('nav a[href^="#"]')];
const map = Object.fromEntries(links.map(l => [l.getAttribute('href').slice(1), l]));

const io = new IntersectionObserver(
  entries => {
    entries.forEach(en => {
      if (!en.isIntersecting) return;
      links.forEach(l => l.classList.remove('active'));
      map[en.target.id]?.classList.add('active');
    });
  },
  { rootMargin: '-12% 0px -78% 0px', threshold: 0 },
);

document.querySelectorAll('section[id]').forEach(s => io.observe(s));
```

The `rootMargin` creates a trigger band roughly 12%–22% from the top of the viewport. A section becomes active when its top edge crosses into that band, so only one link is highlighted at a time and it reflects where the reader actually is. The `active` class is the same one VantaUI's navigation CSS already styles for current-page links.

## Vue 3

```js
import { createApp } from 'vue';
import VantaUI from 'vantaui-css/vue'; // imports CSS, adds .vui to <body>, boots behaviours
import App from './App.vue';
createApp(App).use(VantaUI).mount('#app');
// options: app.use(VantaUI, { behaviours: false, bodyClass: false, root: el })
```

## Nuxt 3

```ts
// nuxt.config.ts
export default defineNuxtConfig({
  modules: ['vantaui-css/nuxt'],
  VantaUI: { behaviours: true, bodyClass: true }, // both default true
});
```

The module registers the stylesheet, adds `.vui` to `<body>` app-wide, and (client-side) boots the optional behaviours.

---

## Common mistakes to avoid

- Do NOT use `<i>` for italics; it renders an icon glyph. Use `<em>` instead.
- Do NOT add component classes like `.panel`, `.card`, or `.btn`. Use the semantic element instead. Helpers are single words, not BEM.
- Do NOT prefix bare semantic usage; the `vui-` prefix is only for free-floating layout/utility/primitive helpers.
- Do NOT use `!important` to override VantaUI. Write an unlayered class, which will always take precedence.
- The package is named `vantaui-css` and must be installed from GitHub because it is not yet available on npm or any CDN.
- The default `main` is the minified CSS; use `vantaui-css/css` for the unminified build.

---

# Extensions (opt-in add-ons)

Add-ons live outside the core bundle, each with its own import, so core stays slim and you ship only what you use. Core reserves an empty `vui.ext` cascade layer (between `components` and `utilities`) that add-ons fill. No JavaScript ships in any add-on: widgets are pure CSS driven by custom properties, and you feed the numbers in.

```js
import 'vantaui-css';            // core
import 'vantaui-css/prose';      // VantaProse: long-form layout
import 'vantaui-css/stats';      // VantaStats: data-viz widgets
```

Plain HTML: link `dist/ext/prose.css` / `dist/ext/stats.css` (or `.min.css`) after the core stylesheet.

## VantaProse (`vantaui-css/prose`)

The `vui-prose` long-form layout: a grid that centers text to a readable measure (`--prose-width`, 65ch) while letting assets bleed wider (`--bleed-width`, 85ch). Bleed wider with `vui-bleed`, `bleed`, or `half-bleed` (`<img>`, `<figure>`, `<blockquote>`, `<pre>` bleed automatically; a lone-image paragraph bleeds too). Edge-to-edge: `vui-bleed-full` / `bleed-full`. Apply to an `<article>`; the prose class opts that article out of panel styling. Migrating from v1.x: add the import, the markup is unchanged.

## VantaStats (`vantaui-css/stats`)

Small chart widgets in the HUD style. Tone any with a color word: cyan (default) · amber · green · red. All classes use the `vui-` prefix.

- **Stat card** (`vui-stat-card`): a headline metric on a standalone chamfered plate. Children: `vui-stat-label` (a `//` system eyebrow), `vui-stat-value` (readout-sized, glows in the tone), `vui-stat-sub`.
- **Bar chart** (`vui-bars`): vertical bars. Each `vui-bar` sets `--v` (height as a 0–1 fraction of the max); children `vui-bar-value`, `vui-bar-fill`, `vui-bar-label`. Track height via `--vui-bars-height`. Empty state: `vui-bars-empty`.
- **Leaderboard** (`vui-leaderboard`, an `<ol>`): ranked `vui-board-row`s. Each holds `vui-board-rank` (add `top` to glow), `vui-board-main` > `vui-board-head` (`vui-board-name` + `vui-board-count`) and a `vui-board-bar` whose `--v` is width as a 0–1 fraction.
- **Activity heatmap** (`vui-heatmap`): a GitHub-style calendar. Emit one `<i style="--level: 0..4">` per day; 7 rows flow into week columns automatically. A single `color-mix` scales fill and glow by `--level`. Empty pad cells: `vui-cell-pad`. Optional `vui-heatmap-legend` with `vui-heat-swatch` samples. Recipe: map `count` to a level (`count===0 ? 0 : Math.ceil(count/max*4)`), pad the first week so day 1 lands on its weekday, emit cells in date order; wrap in an `overflow-x:auto` scroller for a full year.
- **Sparkline** (`vui-sparkline`): styles a consumer-supplied inline `<svg>` (`vui-spark-area` polygon + `vui-spark-line` polyline; `vui-spark-flat` for one point). Non-scaling stroke.
- **Donut / pie** (`vui-donut`): a `conic-gradient` ring. `--vui-donut` is the segment stop list (`color 0 <end%>`, cumulative); `--vui-donut-size` the diameter; `--vui-donut-hole` the inner radius (0% = filled pie). Wrap in `vui-donut-figure` with a `vui-donut-center` readout. The hole uses a radial mask; without masking it degrades to a filled pie.
- **Delta** (`vui-delta`): a trend value with an auto direction glyph. `up` (green ▲), `down` (red ▼), `flat` (muted ─); add `vui-delta-bare` to supply your own glyph.
- **Legend** (`vui-legend`, a `<ul>`): tone-swatch captions; per-item tone word, or override `--_c`.

## Browser support

Evergreen browsers from ~2023: Chrome/Edge 111+, Firefox 121+, Safari 16.4+. The Baseline floor is `:has()`, cascade layers, container queries, `color-mix()`, `oklch()`, `mask`, and `conic-gradient`, all interoperable across those engines.

VantaUI is built CSS-first and works in Firefox and Safari, not only Chrome. Where Chrome ships a newer feature that the others lack (native CSS carousel controls, anchor-positioned popovers, `corner-shape` bevels, animated `<details>`), Chrome uses that superior no-JS path while the other browsers fall back: the feature either degrades to a plain but working state, or, where a plain state would be unusable, the optional JS restores parity (see the carousel `carousels()` behaviour). The rule is CSS-first: JS is only used where CSS cannot do the job yet, and every feature has a fallback.

Contributors: a Baseline gate runs in CI and on pre-commit. `npm run lint` uses ESLint with `@eslint/css` (`use-baseline`, reading the official web-features dataset) for CSS and `eslint-plugin-compat` for JS, so a non-Baseline feature added without a fallback is flagged at the exact line. The known-and-handled features above Baseline are listed with their fallbacks in `eslint.config.js`.

## License

MIT. An original tactical-HUD interpretation with no trademarked marks or character likenesses.
